overall_summary: The ElizaOS project is currently transitioning through a major infrastructure overhaul focused on the 'Eliza Cloud Apps' platform and per-tenant isolation. Development is balancing significant architectural refactoring of monolith plugins with critical bug fixes for production deployment blockers.
key_facts: Naked_monk submitted PR #8294 to integrate the @usenami/plugin-signer into the ElizaOS third-party registry, featuring secure AWS Nitro Enclave key management.
The local gRPC pipeline architecture was proposed by carlgatz to achieve sub-millisecond latency and minimize token costs by filtering data before AI processing.
PR #8277 merged, refactoring the 'plugin-lifeops' monolith into six specialized plugins including plugin-relationships, plugin-inbox, and plugin-goals.
Critical production blockers were identified in Eliza Cloud Apps (Issue #8321), specifically missing ingress installation, Postgres connection ceilings, and host-port collisions.
Developer 'fixer1123' posted a recruitment notice for a communication coordinator to support an international development team's client interactions.
Automated 'USE_SKILL' broker was enabled to support autonomous sub-agent dispatching for monetized-app loops.
A 48-hour turnaround time for third-party plugin registry reviews was confirmed by odilitime.
The team is currently resolving blocked Android build synchronization issues related to web-less APK builds and Capacitor plugin registration.
open_questions: What is the clarify on supplies distribution for holders?
Does the cloud eliza loader correctly map DATABASE_URL to POSTGRES_URL for the actual app image within the isolated database provision?

new_issues_prs.title: release: promote develop → main (Cloud V2 sync)
new_issues_prs.author: standujar
new_issues_prs.significance: Standard promotion to sync production with the major Cloud V2 stack updates.

new_issues_prs.title: refactor(lifeops): split plugin-lifeops into focused plugins
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Major architectural refactor decomposing a 7.5K-LOC monolith to increase modularity.

new_issues_prs.title: Eliza Cloud Apps: scaling + ingress gaps before go-live
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: P0 launch blocker identifying that the system currently only supports dozens of apps rather than the target of 1000.

overall_focus.claim: Development focus on June 11-12 centered on stabilizing the agent orchestrator and cloud infrastructure, managing data-plane private networks via Terraform, and fixing affiliate revenue-share tracking.

discord_updates.summary: High-volume data processing optimization using gRPC and RAM-caching was discussed as a way to circumvent RPC latency and high token costs.
DAPPCRAFT initiated a partnership proposal regarding the connection of two specific products; moderators agreed to a formal review in DMs.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Community members highlighted a need for harder trivia questions in the leaderboard system as users are quickly amassing millions of points.

strategic_insights.insight: The project is actively moving away from monolith plugins (LifeOps) towards domain-specific plugins, which will require developers to update their dependency imports.
implications_or_questions: How will this affect existing third-party plugins looking for the LifeOps anchor?

strategic_insights.insight: There is a strong push for 'AI-lite' architectures (local gRPC, RAM-first logic) to ensure the platform remains economically viable for high-volume blockchain data.
implications_or_questions: Should these gRPC patterns be standardized into a core ElizaOS SDK?

market_analysis.observation: Introduction of Ruby Labs AI, a Web3 initiative aimed at bridging traditional web and onchain users in a novel way.
market_analysis.relevance: Represents a potential integration or competitive bridge for the ElizaOS ecosystem.
